# Arapov Trade

> Arapov Trade (arapov.trade) is a free trading-education platform by Igor Arapov, a practising trader since 2013 and independent researcher in the psychology of investment decisions and behavioral finance. It teaches a single, consistent method — market structure, volume analysis and the Wyckoff approach — across 151 articles, a structured beginner course, and a published book series, in Russian (the primary authoring language), Ukrainian and English.

The content is educational only: it explains methodology, analysis techniques, risk and psychology. It is not financial advice, does not give trade signals or specific investment recommendations, and makes no profit guarantees.

Every article exists in three languages under the path pattern `https://arapov.trade/{ru|uk|en}/freestudying/{slug}`. The links below use the English versions; replace `/en/` with `/ru/` or `/uk/` for the other languages.

## Start Here

- [Free Trading Course for Beginners](https://arapov.trade/en/freestudying/freeeducation): The main pillar course — a full path from terminal basics to a complete trading system, with live chart examples.
- [Self-Study Guide](https://arapov.trade/en/freestudying/selfstudying): How to learn trading independently using the free materials.
- [Trading Basics](https://arapov.trade/en/freestudying/tradingbasics): First concepts for someone with no prior experience.
- [Trading Quick Start](https://arapov.trade/en/freestudying/tradingquickstart): The shortest route into placing a first practice trade.
- [Free Courses Catalog](https://arapov.trade/en/freestudying): Index of all free articles by category.

## Core Method: Volume and Wyckoff

- [Volume Market Analysis](https://arapov.trade/en/freestudying/volmarketanalisys): Reading the balance of buyers and sellers through trade volume.
- [Wyckoff Method](https://arapov.trade/en/freestudying/wyckoffmethod): How large players accumulate and distribute, and the traces they leave.
- [Wyckoff Volume Concepts](https://arapov.trade/en/freestudying/wyckoffsvolumeconcept): The effort-vs-result principle and volume interpretation.
- [Peak Volume Levels](https://arapov.trade/en/freestudying/peakvolumelevels): Identifying climactic activity from professional capital.
- [Order Book Analysis](https://arapov.trade/en/freestudying/stockorderbook): Reading Bid/Ask/Last and pending-order behavior.
- [Market Profile (Steidlmayer)](https://arapov.trade/en/freestudying/steidlmayeranalysis): Auction theory and value-area analysis.

## Technical Analysis

- [Technical Analysis Basics](https://arapov.trade/en/freestudying/technicalanalysis): Market phases, trend and range, and how to read structure.
- [Support and Resistance](https://arapov.trade/en/freestudying/levelofsupport): Building and trading levels.
- [Japanese Candlesticks](https://arapov.trade/en/freestudying/japanesecandle): Bars vs candlesticks and how to read them.
- [Candlestick Patterns](https://arapov.trade/en/freestudying/candlestickpatterns): Price Action patterns and their meaning.
- [Pin Bar Pattern](https://arapov.trade/en/freestudying/pinbar): Reversal pin-bar entries.
- [Fibonacci Levels](https://arapov.trade/en/freestudying/fibonaccilevels): Retracement and extension levels.
- [Elliott Waves](https://arapov.trade/en/freestudying/wavesofelliott): Wave structure of price movement.

## Smart Money Concept

- [Smart Money Concepts Guide](https://arapov.trade/en/freestudying/smartmoneyconceptsguide): How large participants drive price and take retail liquidity.
- [Order Blocks](https://arapov.trade/en/freestudying/orderblockintrading): Institutional order zones.
- [Liquidity Pools](https://arapov.trade/en/freestudying/liquiditypools): Where stop clusters and liquidity sit.
- [Imbalance and FVG](https://arapov.trade/en/freestudying/imbalanceandfvg): Fair value gaps and imbalance.
- [False Breakouts](https://arapov.trade/en/freestudying/falsebreakouts): Identifying the Spring and shakeouts.
- [Stop Hunting](https://arapov.trade/en/freestudying/stophunting): How stops are triggered and used.

## Risk Management and Psychology

- [Capital Management](https://arapov.trade/en/freestudying/capitalmanagement): Risk management as the core of a profitable system.
- [Risk/Reward Ratio](https://arapov.trade/en/freestudying/profitandlossratio): Why 1:3 produces a positive expectancy.
- [Stop Loss](https://arapov.trade/en/freestudying/stoploss): Placing and sizing protective orders.
- [Drawdowns](https://arapov.trade/en/freestudying/drawdowns): Dispersion, losing streaks and surviving them.
- [Psychological Risks](https://arapov.trade/en/freestudying/psychorisks): Cognitive biases that work against retail traders.
- [How Emotions Affect Trading](https://arapov.trade/en/freestudying/emotionsaffect): Fear, greed and discipline.
- [Building a Trading System](https://arapov.trade/en/freestudying/tradingsystem): Assembling structure, volume, entries and exits into one system.

## Markets and Instruments

- [Forex Market](https://arapov.trade/en/freestudying/forexmarket): Currency-pair trading and its specifics.
- [Futures Trading](https://arapov.trade/en/freestudying/futurestrading): Centralized exchanges and transparent volume.
- [Fundamental Analysis](https://arapov.trade/en/freestudying/fundamentalanalysis): Economic factors that move markets.
- [Gold Trading (XAUUSD)](https://arapov.trade/en/freestudying/goldtrading): Trading the gold market.
- [Cryptocurrency Basics](https://arapov.trade/en/freestudying/cryptocurrencybasics): Entry point to crypto markets.
- [Bitcoin Guide](https://arapov.trade/en/freestudying/bitcoin): Bitcoin as a trading instrument.

## Books

- [Trading Psychology: How to Master Your Emotions and Think Like a Professional](https://arapov.trade/en/books/psihologiya-treydinga): On emotions, discipline and the professional mindset (ISBN 979-8-90243-138-1).
- [Trading Fundamentals: Market Basics, Technical Analysis, Volume Analysis](https://arapov.trade/en/books/osnovy-treydinga): Volume 1 of the foundations series (ISBN 979-8-90243-734-5).
- [Analysis Methods: Technical Analysis, Volume Analysis, Practice](https://arapov.trade/en/books/osnovy-treydinga-tom-two): Volume 2, methods and worked examples (ISBN 979-8-90243-755-0).

## Author and Verification

- [ORCID 0009-0003-0430-778X](https://orcid.org/0009-0003-0430-778X): Researcher identifier.
- [Wikidata Q137454477](https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Q137454477): Knowledge Graph entity (also has a Google Knowledge Graph ID).
- [ISNI 0000 0005 2951 8564](https://isni.org/isni/0000000529518564): Library authority record.
- [Open Library OL16073686A](https://openlibrary.org/authors/OL16073686A): Author catalog.
- [Goodreads author](https://www.goodreads.com/author/show/66848566): Reader-facing author catalog.
- [Bowker Bookwire author page](https://bookwire.bowker.com/author/Igor-Arapov-40225801): Permanent author page in the official US ISBN agency catalog (Bowker).
- [Zenodo (CERN)](https://zenodo.org/records/18057306): DOI-registered publications.
- [Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=N440tWQAAAAJ): Citations profile.
- [OpenAlex](https://openalex.org/A5127355048): Scholarly works indexed in the OpenAlex open research graph.
- [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/igor-arapov): Professional profile.
- [YouTube @ArapovTrade](https://www.youtube.com/@arapovtrade): Video lessons.
- [Medium](https://medium.com/@arapov.trade): Articles and notes.
- [TradingView track record](https://arapov.trade/en/freestudying/tradingview-record): Public, documented trading track record on TradingView since 2021.
- [TradingView profile (Igor_Arapov)](https://www.tradingview.com/u/Igor_Arapov/): Primary source of the public analysis since 2021, with charts featured in TradingView editorial picks.
- [Guest lecture at the National University of Food Technologies (NUFT, Kyiv)](https://nuft.edu.ua/news/podiyi/pppro-trejding-i-birzhovu-diyalnist-%E2%80%93-zdobuvacham-osvitnoyi-programi): Independent university record of a 2026 guest lecture on trading and exchange markets.
- [Media profile (ua.news)](https://ua.news/ua/money/nezvazhaiuchi-na-viinu-ta-krizi-igor-arapov-poiasniuie-khto-zavzhdi-zarobliaie-na-fondovikh-rinkakh): Independent news media coverage.
- [Open textbook on Wikibooks](https://uk.wikibooks.org/wiki/%D0%9E%D1%81%D0%BD%D0%BE%D0%B2%D0%B8_%D1%82%D1%80%D0%B5%D0%B9%D0%B4%D0%B8%D0%BD%D0%B3%D1%83): "Trading Fundamentals" open textbook hosted on the Wikimedia Foundation's Wikibooks.
- [Book series in the National Library of Ukraine (Vernadsky)](http://www.irbis-nbuv.gov.ua/cgi-bin/irbis64r_81/cgiirbis_64.exe?Z21ID=&I21DBN=VFEIR&P21DBN=VFEIR&S21STN=1&S21REF=10&S21FMT=fullw&C21COM=S&S21CNR=20&S21P01=3&S21P02=0&S21P03=A=&S21COLORTERMS=0&S21STR=%D0%90%D1%80%D0%B0%D0%BF%D0%BE%D0%B2%2C%20%D0%86%D0%B3%D0%BE%D1%80): Catalog search by author name in the National Library of Ukraine, where the printed "Trading Fundamentals" book series is held.

## Optional

- [Peer-reviewed paper: Psychology of Investment Decisions — Cognitive Biases of Retail Traders](https://doi.org/10.32702/2306-6814.2026.4.96): Journal "Investments: Practice and Experience," No. 4 (2026); co-authored with Prof. Inna Sytnyk.
- [SSRN working paper: From Tilt to System — A Practitioner's Framework for Managing Cognitive Biases in Retail Trading](https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/papers.cfm?abstract_id=6254718): Applied framework for managing cognitive biases in retail trading (SSRN 6254718).
- [Ukrainian site (root domain)](https://arapov.trade): Homepage in Ukrainian.
- [Russian site](https://arapov.trade/ru/main): Homepage in Russian.
- [English site](https://arapov.trade/en/main): Homepage in English.
- [Practical Trade Examples](https://arapov.trade/en/freestudying/practic): Worked examples and range-trading practice.
- [Trading Indicators Overview](https://arapov.trade/en/freestudying/tradingindicators): Why indicators are supplementary, not signals.
- [Full multilingual article map](https://arapov.trade/en/freestudying): Complete catalog of all 151 articles across the three languages.
